phenomenon: moral valence effects on epistemic attribution
claim: Non-epistemic factors like moral status of consequences influence knowledge attribution contrary to epistemological purism.

constructs: perspective-taking cultural distance moral truth relativism
claim: Folk moral intuitions are not stably objectivist but vary systematically with degree of cultural difference considered.
Knowledge ascriptions encode a triadic relation to alternatives rather than a dyadic subject-proposition relation.

phenomenon: bypassing confusion in free will intuitions
constructs: determinism mental causation bypassing modal scope
claim: Apparent incompatibilism reflects misinterpretation of determinism as entailing mental-state irrelevance rather than genuine conceptual incompatibility.

constructs: inhibitory control L1-interference category formation perception production
claim: Ability to suppress L1 phonological activation enhances L2 segment perception more than production accuracy.

phenomenon: modal sensitivity masquerading as stakes sensitivity
constructs: stakes deontic modality knowledge ascription normative reasoning
claim: Apparent effects of practical stakes on knowledge attributions arise from deontic modal interpretation rather than knowledge truth conditions.
